children.text: Consultation by Secretary with Governor, local officials, and affected landowners; notice to Congressional committees; publication in Federal Register
The Secretary of the Interior (hereinafter referred to as the “Secretary”) shall, beginning within thirty days after

children.tail: , consult with the Governor of the State of Washington, the Board of Commissioners of Clallam County, and the affected landowners, and shall locate a boundary encompassing all of the shoreline of Lake Ozette, including privately owned aquatic lands not within the boundary of the park on
, That such boundary shall be located not less than two hundred feet set back from the ordinary high-water mark of Lake Ozette:
, That the privately owned lands encompassed within the park by such boundary shall not exceed one thousand five hundred acres. The Secretary shall, within one hundred and eighty days after

, and following reasonable notice in writing to the Committees on Interior and Insular Affairs of the Senate and House of Representatives of his intention to do so, publish in the Federal Register a detailed description of the boundary located pursuant to this section. Upon such publication the Secretary is authorized to revise the map on file pursuant to
accordingly, and such revised map shall have the same force and effect as if included in sections 251e to 251m of this title.

children.text: Sections 251e to 251m of this title, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning
Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the Senate abolished and replaced by Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate, effective

children.tail: . See Rule XXV of Standing Rules of the Senate, as amended by Senate Resolution No. 4 (popularly cited as the “Committee System Reorganization Amendments of 1977”), approved

children.text: Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the House of Representatives changed to Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives on

children.tail: , by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Third Congress.
